text: Increasing complexity forces the transport sector to increase scale. [Sector forecast](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/sectorprognoses/index.html). [Transport & Logistics](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/logistiek/index.html). 09/12/2025 • 4 minutes reading. [Albert Jan Swart](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/albert-jan-swart/index.html).

metadata.source_title: Increasing complexity forces the transport sector to increase scale

text: After the chaos of 2025 caused by American President Trump, 2026 must be a year of recovery.

metadata.source_title: Increasing complexity forces the transport sector to increase scale

text: The transport sector can benefit from improving international trade and is expected to grow by 2 percent. At the same time, legislation, regulations and technological developments force economies of scale. The number of mergers and acquisitions may therefore increase again. Partly due to staff shortages and low interest rates, the number of mergers and acquisitions in the transport sector peaked in 2022. In 2023 and 2024, the number of mergers and acquisitions decreased considerably. The sharp rise in long-term interest rates and lower profit margins in the sector are putting pressure on valuations. Long-term interest rates have hardly fallen since 2022, which means that the financing costs of an acquisition remain at a reasonably high level. Other macroeconomic conditions appear more favorable. International trade and economic growth can pick up in 2026, partly thanks to growing consumer spending and investments in infrastructure and defense. All sectors can benefit from this, such as road carriers, logistics service providers, inland shipping, maritime shipping, distribution centers and forwarders. The first shock of US President Trump's trade war is leaving the transport sector behind, making business results more stable and making it easier to value companies.

metadata.source_title: Increasing complexity forces the transport sector to increase scale

text: ABN AMRO expects the sector to grow by 2 percent in 2026. In 2027, growth is expected to decrease slightly to 1.5 percent. Consolidation can continue. Various factors could lead to an increase in the number of mergers and acquisitions. Transport companies must focus on higher labor productivity, especially now that wages are rising sharply and clients are paying close attention to costs.

metadata.source_title: Increasing complexity forces the transport sector to increase scale

text: This requires investments, including in software, which not every company can afford.

metadata.source_title: Increasing complexity forces the transport sector to increase scale

text: Another factor is sustainability, partly driven by new legislation and regulations. For example, the sector will have to deal with the truck tax in July 2026, which will lead to higher costs for many companies. Since lower rates apply to vehicles with lower emissions, the levy could lead to accelerated investments in cleaner vehicles, including electric vehicles. This levy requires strategic fleet management and makes investment plans complex. Investments in electric vehicles must also take into account charging times and the availability of charging infrastructure, the construction of which may be hampered by grid congestion.

metadata.source_title: Increasing complexity forces the transport sector to increase scale

text: This increasing complexity places greater demands on management, and larger companies are better able to attract and reward professional managers. A third factor is that important clients sometimes prefer to do business with large suppliers.

metadata.source_title: Increasing complexity forces the transport sector to increase scale

text: For example, Albert Heijn has reduced the number of carriers from nineteen to just four transport companies. The supermarket chain expects this to lead to higher productivity, lower costs and faster electrification of road transport.

metadata.source_title: Increasing complexity forces the transport sector to increase scale

text: About two-thirds (63 percent) of entrepreneurs in the transport sector want to transfer the company to another owner within ten years, but less than half (46 percent) indicate that they have or are already looking for a successor.

metadata.source_title: Increasing complexity forces the transport sector to increase scale

text: This is evident from research by Ipsos on behalf of ABN AMRO.

metadata.source_title: Increasing complexity forces the transport sector to increase scale

text: Many entrepreneurs in the sector (41 percent) would like to transfer the company due to their age. More time for other interests is also a frequently mentioned reason at 32 percent. Other reasons are to ensure continuity (23 percent) and the uncertain future prospects of the company (20 percent). Only one in five (20 percent) of entrepreneurs has made it clear that a family member will be their successor. If no successor is yet in sight, entrepreneurs should think about this seriously now, despite their daily activities. It is advisable to engage an advisor at an early stage.

metadata.source_title: Increasing complexity forces the transport sector to increase scale

text: The market is changing rapidly under the influence of legislation, regulations and technological developments. If the company does not respond sufficiently to this by innovating, there is a risk that over time it will receive a much lower valuation than expected when sold. One of the most frequently mentioned challenges in business succession is letting go of control (20 percent). Yet entrepreneurs must do just that to create space for talented managers from new generations. One step ahead with our sector expertise. It is important for every entrepreneur to know what is going on now, but especially what is to come. Base decisions on developments and trends in the sector. Our sector experts know what is going on. See where the opportunities lie.

metadata.source_title: Increasing complexity forces the transport sector to increase scale

Read more in the transport and logistics sector. After a difficult year, in which international trade declined due to a rapid reduction of excess inventories, the transport sector has started a cautious recovery. At the beginning of 2024, freight volumes increased in both container shipping and aviation.

metadata.source_title: Increasing complexity forces the transport sector to increase scale

text:
Thanks to the improving economic growth, the sector can recover further. However, the recovery is being held back by lower demand for building materials. In the meantime, road transport will have to deal with a pile-up of environmental regulations in the coming years.
